senses.gloss: paper; a sheet of paper, a page

examples.text: وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ كِتَابًا فِي قِرْطَاسٍ فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ لَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هَذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ
examples.english: And even if we had sent down to you some writing on a leaf so that they could touch it with their own hands, those who have disbelieved would say, “This is nothing but blatant magic."

examples.text:
10th century, Al-Mutanabbi
اَلْخَيْلُ وَٱللَّيْلُ وَٱلْبَيْدَاءُ تَعْرِفُنِي / وَٱلسَّيْفُ وَٱلرُّمْحُ وَٱلْقِرْطَاسُ وَٱلْقَلَمُ
al-ḵaylu wa-l-laylu wa-l-baydāʔu taʕrifunī / wa-s-sayfu wa-r-rumḥu wa-l-qirṭāsu wa-l-qalamu
examples.english: The horses and the night and the desert know me / and the sword and the spear and the paper and the pen

examples.text: ولكنّني حين رأيتُ هذه الأصناف القديمة سال لعابي، واشتهيتُ أكلها وكأنّني طفل. مزّقتُ قرطاس البسكويت الأول والتهمته بتلذذ،
examples.english: But when I saw these old sorts my spittle ran, and I was desirous to eat thereof as though I were a child. I rent the paper packaging of the first biscuit and devoured with winne,

senses.gloss: a mark that is fired at (such as a bullseye), a target

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Ancient Greek χάρτης (khártēs)bor.
Aramaic קַרְטִיסָא /ܩܲܪܛܝܼܣܵܐ (qarṭīsā)bor.
Arabic قرطاس
From Aramaic קַרְטִיסָא / ܩܲܪܛܝܼܣܵܐ (qarṭīsā), from Ancient Greek χάρτης (khártēs).
